informal: Prove that quotient groups of a solvable group are solvable.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_3_4_5b {G : Type*} [Group G] [IsSolvable G]
  (H : Subgroup G) [Normal H] :
  IsSolvable (G ⧸ H)
reasoning: The informal statement asserts that any quotient of a solvable group is solvable. The Lean signature takes an arbitrary group G with [IsSolvable G] and an arbitrary normal subgroup H, then concludes IsSolvable (G ⧸ H). Quotient groups of G are precisely G ⧸ H for normal subgroups H, so the universal quantification over quotients is correctly captured by the universally quantified G and H. The hypotheses (solvable, normal) and conclusion (solvability of the quotient) match exactly, with no extra or missing assumptions.
